## 5.3 Model Solutions (Step-by-Step)

### Model Solution for Question A

#### (a) Modbus Network Architecture

**Block Diagram:**

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    M["MASTER PC OR PLC"]
    M --- BUS["SERIAL BUS RS-485 TWISTED PAIR"]
    BUS --- S1["SLAVE 1 ADDR 0X01"]
    BUS --- S2["SLAVE 2 ADDR 0X02"]
    BUS --- S3["SLAVE 3 ADDR 0X03"]
    BUS --- SN["SLAVE 247 ADDR 0XF7"]
```

**Key Points:**

- **Master Role**: Initiates all transactions, polls slaves cyclically, processes responses, and detects timeouts.
- **Slave Role**: Listens passively; responds only when addressed. Cannot initiate communication.
- **Address Scheme**: 1–247 valid; 0 is reserved for **broadcast** (no slave may respond to broadcast; only write commands allowed).
- **Rules**:
  1. Only one Master per bus segment.
  2. Slaves may not communicate peer-to-peer.
  3. The Master must enforce a **silent interval** of 3.5 char-times between frames to allow bus settling.
  4. The Master is responsible for **error recovery** and re-tries.

#### (b) CRC-16 Derivation for `0x01 0x03 0x00 0x6B 0x00 0x02`

**Step 1 — Pre-load the CRC register**
$$CRC = 0xFFFF$$

**Step 2 — Process byte 0x01**

| Shift | LSB | Operation | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Init | — | 0xFFFF ⊕ 0x01 | 0xFFFE |
| 1 | 0 | >> 1 | 0x7FFF |
| 2 | 1 | (>> 1) ⊕ 0xA001 | 0xBFFE ⊕ 0xA001 = 0x1FFF |
| 3 | 1 | (>> 1) ⊕ 0xA001 | 0x0FFF ⊕ 0xA001 = 0xAFFE |
| 4 | 0 | >> 1 | 0x57FF |
| 5 | 1 | (>> 1) ⊕ 0xA001 | 0x2BFF ⊕ 0xA001 = 0x8BFE |
| 6 | 0 | >> 1 | 0x45FF |
| 7 | 1 | (>> 1) ⊕ 0xA001 | 0x22FF ⊕ 0xA001 = 0x82FE |
| 8 | 0 | >> 1 | 0x417F |

**Step 3 — Process subsequent bytes (0x03, 0x00, 0x6B, 0x00, 0x02)** using the identical 8-shift loop, propagating the resulting CRC value as the new initial seed.

**Step 4 — After processing all 6 bytes, the canonical CRC-16 of the message `01 03 00 6B 00 02` is:**
$$CRC = 0xB880$$

**Step 5 — Transmit low-byte first, then high-byte:**
$$\text{Tx bytes: } \texttt{0x80 0xB8}$$

**Final RTU Frame on the wire:**
$$\texttt{01 03 00 6B 00 02 80 B8}$$

### Model Solution for Question B

#### (a) Modbus RTU Frame Format

**Query frame** (Master → Slave):

| Address | Function | Start Addr Hi | Start Addr Lo | Quantity Hi | Quantity Lo | CRC Lo | CRC Hi |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B |

**Response frame** (Slave → Master):

| Address | Function | Byte Count | Data[1] | … | Data[N] | CRC Lo | CRC Hi |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B | … | 1 B | 1 B | 1 B |

**Silent Interval — 3.5 Char Times:**

A character at 9600 baud with 8-N-1 (11 bits per char) takes:
$$t_{char} = \frac{11}{9600} = 1.146 \text{ ms}$$

The silent interval is therefore:
$$t_{gap} = 3.5 \times 1.146 = 4.01 \text{ ms}$$

This gap is **greater than the longest inter-character time** of 1.5 char-times. A receiver can therefore unambiguously detect:
- **Frame boundary** — gap ≥ 3.5 char-times ⇒ end of frame.
- **Inter-character gap** — gap < 1.5 char-times ⇒ characters belong to the same frame.

This prevents a slave from responding to a **partial/corrupt** frame and ensures **bus arbitration** on the multi-drop line.

#### (b) ASCII Frame with LRC for Read 3 Registers from 0x006B

**Step 1 — List the message bytes (address + function + data):**

| Byte | Value |
|---|---|
| Slave Address | 0x03 |
| Function Code | 0x03 |
| Address High | 0x00 |
| Address Low | 0x6B |
| Quantity High | 0x00 |
| Quantity Low | 0x03 |

**Step 2 — Sum all six bytes:**
$$S = 0x03 + 0x03 + 0x00 + 0x6B + 0x00 + 0x03 = 0x74$$

**Step 3 — Compute the two's complement:**
$$LRC = 0x100 - 0x74 = 0x8C$$

**Step 4 — Construct the ASCII frame with each byte as 2 hex characters:**

| `:` | 0 | 3 | 0 | 3 | 0 | 0 | 6 | B | 0 | 0 | 0 | 3 | 8 | C | CR | LF |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Start | Slave | FC | AddrHi | AddrLo | QtyHi | QtyLo | LRC | End | | | | | | | | |

**Final transmitted ASCII string:**
$$\texttt{:0303006B00038C\r\n}$$

---

## 5.4 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning

> [!WARNING] Common Mistakes That Cost Marks
> 1. **Forgetting the LRC is appended as 2 ASCII hex characters**, not as a single hex value. A `0x8C` LRC must be written as `'8' 'C'`, not as `0x8C` (3 marks lost if omitted).
> 2. **CRC-16 must be transmitted LOW byte first, then HIGH byte** — the reverse order of normal big-endian. Examiners specifically check this.
> 3. **Modbus RTU silent interval = 3.5 character times**, not 3.5 ms in general; the time depends on the baud rate. A student writing "3.5 ms" without mentioning baud loses credit.
> 4. **Broadcast address 0 is valid only for write function codes (05, 06, 15, 16).** Slaves never respond to broadcast — a student saying "all slaves will reply to address 0" loses 1 mark.
> 5. **Modbus TCP uses port 502** but does **not** include a CRC; it relies on TCP's checksum. Do not include a CRC field in a Modbus-TCP frame.
> 6. **Register width is 16 bits**, not 8 bits. Students referring to "1-byte Modbus registers" lose 1 mark.

---

## 5.5 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT] Rapid-Revision Checklist
> - **Origin**: Modicon (Schneider Electric), **1979**, originally for PLCs.
> - **Topology**: Single-Master, multi-Slave (up to 247); Master initiates, Slaves only respond.
> - **Three Variants**: **RTU** (binary, CRC-16, 3.5 char-time gaps), **ASCII** (printable, LRC, `:`+CRLF delimiters), **TCP** (Ethernet, port 502, MBAP header 7 bytes, no checksum).
> - **Four Data Tables**: Discrete Inputs, Coils, Input Registers, Holding Registers — accessed by FC 01/02/03/04 (read) and 05/06/15/16 (write).
> - **Function Code Encoding**: Public codes 1–127; Exception responses set the MSB → FC+0x80.
> - **Exception Codes**: 1 = Illegal Function, 2 = Illegal Data Address, 3 = Illegal Data Value.
> - **LRC Formula**: $LRC = 256 - (\sum \text{bytes} \mod 256)$; 8-bit two's complement.
> - **CRC-16**: Polynomial 0xA001 (reflected), init 0xFFFF, 8-shift-per-byte loop, transmitted **low byte first**.
> - **Silent Interval**: 3.5 character times at the chosen baud rate marks frame boundary.
> - **Maximum ADU**: 256 bytes (serial) / 260 bytes (TCP including MBAP).
> - **Port 502** is the IANA-assigned well-known port for Modbus-TCP.
> - **Virtual Instrumentation relevance**: LabVIEW's Modbus Library, NI-VISA, and SCADA VIs use Modbus as the standard data-exchange protocol with field instruments.
> - **Engineering Applications**: SCADA, energy metering, HVAC, motor drives (VFDs), PLC inter-communication, IIoT gateways, smart sensors.
